commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=953f5bd6cf324079928eea8e16c4f71f348cd4ae)
commands.review_summary:
The PR migrates shell completion, systemd linger, and startup channel Doctor maintenance repairs onto the structured health-check runner path with focused tests and related Doctor contribution wiring.

Reproducibility: Partly: this PR is a refactor rather than a bug report, but the dry-run preview issue is source-reproducible because the systemd repair dry-run branch ignores the detected finding kind. I did not run a live systemd scenario.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor real-behavior proof and maintainer sequencing review; automation cannot supply the contributor's CLI environment proof.

target.title: Doctor: structure core maintenance repairs
target.body:
## Summary

Builds on the merged Doctor runner normalization from #83753 by moving three core maintenance repairs onto the structured Doctor runner path:

- shell completion detection/repair now returns structured findings, changes, and quiet no-op behavior for declined optional installs
- systemd linger detection/repair now returns structured findings and repair results while still running after Gateway service repair can establish the systemd precondition
- startup channel maintenance now runs through the structured repair bridge, with no normal lint finding and dry-run/effect preview support

The public plugin health contract stays unchanged. This PR keeps `run(ctx)` as an internal core runner shape only; public/plugin checks remain split `detect(ctx, scope?)` plus optional `repair(ctx, findings)`.

The structured bridge now preserves existing `doctor --fix` coverage for all registered repairable checks. Plain `openclaw doctor` diagnostics are scoped to the newly converted checks so the removed legacy contribution slots keep reporting without duplicating unrelated existing surfaces.

This also makes the existing plugin activation boundary test pass its local manifest fixture directly to model normalization, avoiding full-shard order dependence from global plugin metadata state.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental false --pretty false`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental false --pretty false`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.test.ts src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts --reporter=dot --pool=threads --fileParallelism=false` — 8 files / 63 tests passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-activation-boundary.test.ts --reporter=dot --pool=threads --fileParallelism=false` — 1 file / 1 test passed
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1` on the touched files — passed
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint` on the touched files — 0 warnings / 0 errors
- `codex review --base origin/main` — clean after accepting and fixing the repair coverage finding

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: Core Doctor maintenance repairs for shell completion, systemd linger, and startup channel maintenance now use the structured Doctor runner path while preserving plain Doctor diagnostics, repair-mode mutation, dry-run previews, and quiet optional no-op behavior.

Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu 24.04 source checkout, rebased onto `origin/main` after #83753 landed.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Rebased onto `origin/main`, typechecked core/test projects, ran the focused Doctor Vitest suite covering shell completion, systemd linger, startup maintenance, contribution ordering, conversion inventory, lint flow, and repair flow; then ran the plugin activation boundary regression test, formatting, oxlint, and Codex autoreview.

Evidence after fix: Focused Vitest passed for 8 Doctor files / 63 tests; plugin activation boundary test passed for 1 file / 1 test; core test-project `tsgo` passed; core production `tsgo` passed; oxfmt passed on touched files; direct oxlint reported 0 warnings and 0 errors; `codex review --base origin/main` returned no actionable regressions after the all-registered-repairs fix.

Observed result after fix: Converted checks remain visible during plain Doctor diagnostics, mutate only through repair mode, avoid duplicate legacy contribution prompts, run systemd linger after Gateway service repair, keep optional shell completion declines quiet, and preserve existing registered `doctor --fix` repairs outside the three converted checks.

What was not tested: No full `pnpm check`, no full Vitest suite, and no remote Crabbox/Testbox run for this PR2 refresh yet.
target.author: giodl73-repo

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
